programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: Police suspect a serial killer when two women and their teenage daughters are murdered in Maryland during the search for clues, it's revealed that a master thief is the key to solving the mystery.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: The case of Johnny Hincapie, whose conviction for the 1990 murder of a Utah tourist in New York -- a case that represented a tipping point in the city's law enforcement -- was overturned after 25 years.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: Police investigate whether the murder of a South Florida attorney is connected to a web of fraud, betrayal and more than $1 billion in missing money.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: When remains are found amid the rubble after a house fire, authorities soon discover the incident was no accident.

programme.title: Storms of the Century
programme.desc: Since the turn of the millennium, we're seeing deadly and destructive hurricanes slam into the U-S, leaving an unimaginable toll. "Once-in-a-century" storms are now becoming recurring events. Over the next 30 minutes, we are looking back at some of the worst hurricanes starting with a storm that changed New Orleans, and the country, forever - Hurricane Katrina. Hosted by Al Roker.

programme.title: Palisades and Eaton Fires: Devastation to Determination
programme.desc: A look at the January 2025 wildfire disaster that forever changed lives in Altadena and Palisades, how the tragedies altered the landscape of Southern California and what's next in the process of recovery and rebuilding.

programme.title: Survivorman
programme.desc: Les offers his best tips and tricks that he has created over his extensive career of surviving.

programme.title: Survivorman
programme.desc: Danger is inherent in any survival situation. It's how you handle it that determines success or failure.

programme.title: Survivorman
programme.desc: Counting down wilderness survival issues he has faced, Les shows segments of his shows relevant to each item.

programme.title: Survivorman
programme.desc: Les meets with a female African Shaman to experience the rite of passage known as scarification.

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: After submarine exploration of Grand Cayman, George dives with Canadian Armed Forces' deep pressure unit.

programme.title: Experiencing Canada
programme.desc: Discover British Columbia with Nathan as he cycles, tastes local wines, and explores the Osoyoos Desert.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Grève dans le secteur public : les écoles devraient-elles être un service essentiel?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Meeker Guerrier, François Lambert, Victor Henriquez et Geneviève Pettersen.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Administration municipale: les voyages d'affaires devraient-ils être réservés aux élus?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Meeker Guerrier, Justine McIntyre et Robert Poëti.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Devrait-on rendre gratuits les transports en commun au Québec?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Geneviève Pettersen, Patrick Bonin de Greenpeace Canada, Déborah Cherenfant et Justine McIntyre.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Devrait-on interdire l'écriture inclusive dans les communications officielles?
programme.desc: Nos débatteuses : Maude Goyer, Catherine Beauchamp, Jacinthe-Ève Arel et l'enseignante et chroniqueuse en éducation Claudine Potvin.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Don de sperme : le Canada devrait-il rémunérer les donneurs pour mieux encadrer la pratique?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Me Anne-France Goldwater, Dr Alain Vadeboncoeur, François Lambert et Déborah Cherenfant.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Hausse des événements haineux : devrait-on interdire les manifestations en lien avec le conflit Israël-Hamas?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Geneviève Pettersen, Rodolphe Husny, Alex Perron et Antonine Yaccarini.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Est-ce le rôle du gouvernement de subventionner le sport professionnel?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Antonine Yaccarini, Ray Lalonde et Meeker Guerrier.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Doit-on rentrer dans un moule pour faire de la politique?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Victor Henriquez, Antonine Yaccarini, Geneviève Pettersen et Jacinthe-Eve Arel.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Fraudes bancaires : est-ce que les banques nous protègent assez?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Catherine Beauchamp, Victor Henriquez et Alex Perron.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Croyez-vous que la grève permettra aux employés de l'État d'améliorer leurs conditions de travail?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Antonine Yaccarini, Déborah Cherenfant et l'enseignante et chroniqueuse en éducation Claudine Potvin.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Crise du logement : devrait-on interdire les locations d'appartement à court terme?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Victor Henriquez, Geneviève Pettersen, Déborah Cherenfant et l'analyste de la politique municipale Justine McIntyre.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: L'industrie du potin artistique est-elle encore utile?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Geneviève Pettersen, Alex Perron, Catherine Beauchamp et Varda Étienne.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: La parité en politique passe-t-elle par une exclusion temporaire des candidats hommes?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Antonine Yaccarini, Me Alexis Wawanoloath, Rodolphe Husny et notre invitée, Maïka Sondarjée.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: La religion devrait-elle être exclue des funérailles et hommages offerts par l'État?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Déborah Cherenfant, Victor Henriquez, François Lambert et Alex Perron.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre les partys de bureau pour Noël?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Antonine Yaccarini, Victor Henriquez, François Lambert et Maude Goyer.

programme.title: Man Of The Year
programme.desc: Tom Dobbs (Robin Williams), the satirical host of a political talk show, decides to shake things up by running for president after making comments that he would be a better president than the leader who currently occupies the White House. His fans begin a grassroots campaign that catapults him into the Oval Office, but soon after moving in it is revealed a computer glitch mistakenly awarded him the presidency. Tom is left with a tough choice, stay the course or head back behind the microphone.

programme.title: Quicksand
programme.desc: Banker Martin Raikes (Michael Keaton) heads to Monaco to investigate strange financial activity surrounding a film production that his bank is financing. There he encounters the film's star, the washed-up, hard-drinking one-time action star Jake Mellows (Michael Caine). Before he can conduct a thorough investigation, Raikes finds himself framed for the assassination of a government official. With both crooks and the law out to get him, Raikes goes on the run, eventually hooking up with Mellows.

programme.title: Jane Austen's Emma
programme.desc: A beautiful girl is happy to play matchmaker but fails to notice that her own heart has been captured.

programme.title: Labyrinth
programme.desc: Sixteen-year-old Sarah must solve a labyrinth to rescue her baby brother when he is taken by the Goblin King.

programme.title: Avalanche (1999)
programme.desc: The survivor of an Alaskan avalanche, Dr. Lia Freeman (Caroleen Feeney) is an environmentalist concerned about a similar disaster occurring if a proposed new oil pipeline is built. Freeman adamantly warns the planners of the likelihood of a major avalanche, but they ignore her pleas. As she realizes that a massive whiteout is imminent, Freeman tries to get the citizens of a nearby town to leave, aided by brave helicopter pilot Neal Meekin (Thomas Ian Griffith).

programme.title: American Yakuza 2: Back to Back
programme.desc: The violent impulses of Bob Malone (Michael Rooker), a former Los Angeles policeman forced to leave his job in disgrace, get the better of him during a bank robbery, and he wipes out nearly all the robbers. Meanwhile, Koji (Ryo Ishibashi), a Yakuza killer in town to meet with the local Mafia, turns the tables on a deranged terrorist who threatens to bomb an eatery. Corrupt cops bring Koji in, but he soon escapes from custody -- taking Malone and his daughter, Chelsea (Danielle Harris), hostage.

programme.title: Avenging Angelo
programme.desc: A woman discovers she is the daughter of a murdered mafia chieftain and seeks revenge with the aid of her father's faithful bodyguard (Academy Award nominee Sylvester Stallone, "Rocky").

programme.title: A Soldier's Revenge
programme.desc: Haunted by wartime horrors, Civil War soldier-turned-bounty hunter Frank Connor spends his postwar time polishing off two things: whiskey and fugitives.

programme.title: Match à Manhattan
programme.desc: Lorsque Celeste est licenciée, son patron la propose pour un emploi comme au pair chez un célèbre magnat de l'immobilier new-yorkais.

programme.title: Coeur volé
programme.desc: Melissa est enrôlée par le PDG de la plus grande compagnie de bijoux de Miami pour l'aider à créer une ligne pour les mariées.

programme.title: Cuisine, amour, Londres
programme.desc: Lorsque tout ce que Stella croyait acquis s'effondre, elle accepte un poste de chef dans un nouveau restaurant londonien.

programme.title: Amour, set et match
programme.desc: Liz, une enseignante au secondaire, met ses élèves au défi de créer des plans d'affaires : ils lancent une application de rencontre.

programme.title: Un amour d'animateur
programme.desc: Savannah est engagée pour redorer le blason d'un animateur de radio à New York. Elle se retrouve accidentellement à l'antenne avec celui-ci.

programme.title: The Three Stooges In Orbit
programme.desc: The Three Stooges are tangling with Martians.

programme.title: Good Neighbor Sam
programme.desc: A married advertising agent helps out his single neighbor by posing as her husband so she can inherit money.

programme.title: The Harder They Fall
programme.desc: Humphrey Bogart's final screen appearance as a cynical sportswriter involved in the boxing racket.

programme.title: The Goode Family
programme.desc: When Helen discovers that Bliss' community service job is bogus, she tries to give Bliss some actual work to do.

programme.title: The Goode Family
programme.desc: Gerald protests a restaurant after discovering there is meat in their "meatless" chili Che disappears.

programme.title: The Goode Family

programme.desc: Ije tells Sogeum of his plans to gain power through the VIPs in the prison's Special Sector. Sogeum, however, is unimpressed with Ije's intentions. Minsik and Ije each attempt to get the list of investors before the other does.

programme.title: Kangchi, The Beginning Ep.22
programme.desc: Kang Chi visits Monk So Jung, saying he wants to find the Gu Family Book so he can grow old with Yeo Wool. So Jung reveals their fate: one of them must die. Kang Chi is shocked. Meanwhile, Tae Seo is captured and taken to Jo Gwan Woong.

programme.title: Kangchi, The Beginning Ep.23
programme.desc: Afraid of losing Yeo Wool, Kang Chi breaks up with her. She holds onto him, saying that losing him is what she fears most. Upset by the breakup, Yeo Wool goes out to clear her head and is attacked by a mysterious figure.

programme.title: Kangchi, The Beginning Ep.24
programme.desc: While trying to save Kang Chi, Yeo Wool is shot by Seo-bwagwan. Yi Sun Shin arrests Jo Gwan Woong for leaking military secrets. As Yeo Wool dies from the gunshot, Kang Chi promises they will meet again.

programme.title: The Slave Hunters Ep.1
programme.desc: Joseon's notorious slave chaser and money hungry Daegil, find a woman he has been searching for 10 years. She is a slave named Eonnyeon who is directly involved with the fall of Daegil's family.

programme.title: The Slave Hunters Ep.2
programme.desc: After running away from Daegil's house 10 years ago, she changed her name to Hyewon. Seonghwan sets Hyewon up to marry a widowed rich man named Choi.

programme.title: The Slave Hunters Ep.3
programme.desc: Daegil and Taeha engage in a duel. Taeha saves Hyewon from getting raped. Eopbok, who was forced to return as a slave because of Daegil.

programme.title: Do You Like Brahms? Ep.12
programme.desc: Song Ah has to look for a new accompanist when her last one quits. She asks her acquaintances for help, but she has no luck. Just then, Joon Young offers to play for her. People find out that Song Ah and Joon Young are dating and that Joon Young is playing for Song Ah's grad school exam. Rumors about them spread like wildfire, which makes Song Ah doubt if she made the right decision. Meanwhile, Joon Young looks for paid work performing at a concert.

programme.title: Do You Like Brahms? Ep.13
programme.desc: With Jung Kyung constantly asking Joon Young whether he still has feelings for her, Joon Young once again tells her how he feels. Later, Song Ah questions Joon Young about his whereabouts last night after seeing him and Jung Kyung ride off in a taxi. When Director Nah asks Joon Young a favor regarding Jung Kyung, he turns down her request. Meanwhile, Song Ah is going through a hard time herself and makes a decision that should've been made a long time ago.

programme.title: Do You Like Brahms? Ep.14
programme.desc: The recording of Yoo Tae Jin playing "Traumerei" goes viral. However, it turns out that Joon Young performed the music, not Tae Jin. He demands Tae Jin take the recording down, but he refuses. Jung Kyung helps Joon Young by successfully convincing Tae Jin to take the recording down. Song Ah runs into Jung Kyung and hears about the recording and how much it means to Joon Young. In the end, she decides to break up with Joon Young.

programme.title: Do You Like Brahms? Ep.15
programme.desc: After breaking up with Joon Young, Song Ah continues with her entrance exam as if nothing happened. On the other hand, Joon Young feels very bad that he hurt Song Ah. In spite of the dissuasion, Joon Young decides to quit playing the piano. Meanwhile, Director Nah passes away. At the funeral, when Song Ah tells Joon Young about her recital and her plans as a violinist, Joon Young asks her if he can be her accompanist.

programme.title: Do You Like Brahms? Ep.16
programme.desc: While Joon Young continues to wait for Song Ah's answer, Joon Young, Jung Kyung, and Hyeon Ho perform together at Director Nah's commemoration concert. Later, Song Ah shares the news regarding her admission offer from the graduate school with Joon Young. As Song Ah listens attentively to Joon Young's piano performance at his recital, she realizes that her feelings toward Joon Young are solid and carefully opens up to him once again.

programme.title: I Can Hear Your Voice Ep.1
programme.desc: There's a boy who can hear peoples thoughts. 10 years ago, a boy witnessed his father getting murdered during a car accident. The accident was to frame a murder. Since then, he can read the minds of other people by making eye contact with them.

programme.title: LIES HIDDEN IN MY GARDEN (Dubbed) Ep.1

Then, Sonia and Matt use the cover of night to sneak away a Nissan. Too bad the owner is throwing a party with his neck-biting, vampire-wannabe friends. And finally, Matt and Froy face the fury of a clan of drunken relatives when they interrupt a raucous family reunion.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Great Escape S01 E01
programme.desc: Gordon's odyssey begins in the north, home of our curry house classics. On board an express train he's thrown in at the deep end and experiences a back-to-basics baptism of fire, cooking for its many passengers in the train's cramped pantry car and getting travel sick while he's making curry. In stark contrast he then meets his match working for a cantankerous royal chef, preparing a biryani with a difference for a huge wedding feast before venturing into the jungle where he gets eaten alive by ants when he scales a 40-foot tree in search of the local tribe's favourite delicacy. Then it's on to Delhi to cook at the restaurant that invented tandoori chicken, where Gordon puts into practice everything he's learnt for a tough crowd of Delhi food critics.

programme.desc: Every year, a handful of hunters draw a mountain goat permit to hunt the Mills Creek region of Alaska‚ Kenai Peninsula. Steven Rinella holds one of the permits this year, and he and his brother Danny backpack into the mountains, into rugged country they‚ never seen, in hopes of finding a billy goat.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: The Roughest Country: Texas Aoudad Sheep
programme.desc: Steven Rinella heads into the mountainous desert backcountry of west Texas after Barbary sheep, or Aoudad. These introduced sheep from North Africa are hard to find and harder to hunt. Challenged by the harsh environment, rugged terrain, and elusive sheep, Steve finds this hunt to be one of the most difficult he has ever undertaken.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Canyon Bottoms and Cookouts: Texas Javelina
programme.desc: Steven Rinella takes to the canyons of the West Texas desert in search of javelina. In this broken, rocky terrain finding these unique critters isn't easy and getting a clear shot on a skunk pig with a bow is a whole other story. But Steve is inspired by the evidence of he finds of ancient hunters that used the area to prepare a meal that honors both the animal and our hunting ancestors.

programme.desc: Steven Rinella heads to Wisconsin to spend a few days on his buddys historical family farm to hunt small game.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Big Sky, Bigger Birds: Montana Wild Turkey
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and his brother Matt load up the llamas for a backcountry spring Turkey hunt in Montana's Powder River Breaks. They will pack deep into the rugged badlands to find gobbling birds. With two tags each, there's plenty of action and camp-fried turkey schnitzel is on the menu.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: The Fair Chase: Arizona Mountain Lion
programme.desc: In the harsh desert Southwest, Steven Rinella has come to learn the truth about tracking a mountain lions.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: Steven Rinella heads into the mountains of the Sonora Desert on a true, wild, free-ranging bison hunt.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Strange Hunt in a Strange Land: New Zealand Red Stag
programme.desc: Steven Rinella is on New Zealand's South Island for a do-it-yourself, free-range hunt for the majestic red stag. But first, he needs to secure some camp meat in the form of wild lamb. Battling weather and wary game in mountainous terrain, he uses his spot-and-stalk hunting skills to discover Kiwi red stags are a trophy worthy of the wall and the cook pot.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: Bush Pig: New Zealand Wild Boar
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and local pig hunting expert, Darren Moore, spend a wild, action-packed day tracking down Captain Cook boars in the thick New Zealand bush. Their tools for the job include two tough little pig dogs, a knife, and some real grit. Wild pigs are dangerous critters and this is as visceral as a hunt gets. Along the way, Steve learns a new technique for field-prepping whole hogs.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: The Top of the Bottom of the World: New Zealand Thar Part 1
programme.desc: With the help of guide and friend Remi Warren, Steven Rinella gears up for a massive climb into New Zealand's Southern Alps in pursuit of Chamois and Tahr. The hunters will travel light and base out of an alpine hut to find animals Steve has never hunted. Things turns adventurous quickly with an impassable glacial river between Steve and his goal. But with hard work, Steve experiences a do-it-yourself high country hunt like no other.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.sub-title: The Top of the Bottom of the World: New Zealand Thar Part 2
programme.desc: With the help of guide and friend Remi Warren, Steven Rinella gears up for a massive climb into New Zealand's Southern Alps in pursuit of Chamois and Tahr. The hunters will travel light and base out of an alpine hut to find animals Steve has never hunted. Things turns adventurous quickly with an impassable glacial river between Steve and his goal. But with hard work, Steve experiences a do-it-yourself high country hunt like no other.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: The Pilgrims didn't get their turkey at the store and neither does Steven Rinella. On this special Holiday episode Steve cooks three enticing dishes: Wild Turkey Galantine, Smoked Black Bear Ham, and his take on the traditional Mincemeat Pie. Appetites will run large as Steve makes a special meal for his family and friends.

programme.title: Fred Klett
programme.desc: Fred Klett has built a career from being a clean comedian, a style that is his trademark. His comedy is G-rated and focused to appeal to families with members of all ages since much of his material is taken from his experiences growing up in a large Christian family. As a mainstream performer, his brand of humor is rare and refreshing and is rooted in his faith in Christ and belief in the Holy Scriptures.

programme.title: Frances Dilorenzo
programme.desc: Frances takes the stage full of energy and ready for fun. Frances does one thing you don't see many female comics do…physical humor, and she delivers it with every silly bone in her body. Her material stems from her life, but she draws both men and women into her world with such a connection, it feels like she is personalizing every joke. In addition to touring nationally, Frances Dilorinzo's credits include appearances on Nick Mom Night Out – Nickelodeon, Last Comic Standing - NBC, Comics Unleashed -ABC, Secret Life of a Soccer Mom -TLC, Real Housewives of Orange County – Bravo, as well as, performing for our troops around the world.

programme.title: Dennis Gaxiola
programme.desc: Dennis Gaxiola is one of America's funniest clean comedians. The preacher's son gained notoriety from his appearances with Jamie Foxx on Comedy Central, The Latin Kings of Comedy Tour with Paul Rodriguez, BET's Comic View, Que Locos and the 2010 season Martin Lawrence's First Amendment Stand Up. Comedy is Dennis' gift, but his relationship with Jesus Christ is the driving force in his life. A powerful testimony of redemption, healing in marriage and a personal walk with Jesus has lead to Dennis being able minister at Youth Conventions, Marriage Seminars as well as your weekly church service.

programme.desc: Coyote reels in a record-breaking sailfish and takes viewers on a high-seas journey filled with education, adrenaline, and ocean conservation.Looks can be deceiving. Dumb cane, a common plant, releases chemicals that scorch skin. Coyote finds out the hard way.

programme.desc: In a fairytale-worthy twist, Coyote kisses a giant frog in the name of science and fun. Does it turn into royalty? You'll have to see.A sobering episode recounts a fatal snake bite case. Coyote shares what went wrong and what to do in the event of a venomous encounter.

programme.title: Bitten by a Tarantula!
programme.desc: Mark gets up close with a giant tarantula. Though terrifying to many, he demonstrates how docile and misunderstood these spiders truly are.Ants attack! Coyote puts his feet directly into a swarm of biting ants and records just how painful and rapid their assault can be.

programme.title: Stung by the Gympie Gympie! (World's Most Painful Plant Sting)
programme.desc: Coyote braves the Gympie Gympie, Australia's infamous pain plant. The sting leaves lasting effects and is known to bring grown men to tears.With nothing but his bare hands, Coyote tries catfish noodling—reaching into muddy water for a hidden monster. It's wild, messy, and risky.

programme.title: Yuck! What Creature Made This Slime?!
programme.desc: Mystery slime appears on the forest floor—what creature left it behind? The team investigates this sticky situation.As part of #CampYouTube, Mark builds a tide pool aquarium and fills it with sea creatures from the coast—perfect for learning from home.

programme.title: $100 Coffee vs. 1¢ Coffee – Which Actually Tastes Better?
programme.desc: Is a $100 coffee really better than a $0.01 cup? Coyote hosts a wild taste test to find out if price truly equals flavor. BOOM! Coyote gets hit by a surprise blast during filming. He's okay—but the slow-motion replay says otherwise.

programme.title: Diving with a Jetpack!
programme.desc: Coyote tests an underwater jetpack, soaring through the ocean and meeting sea life in this high-speed dive adventure you have to see to believe!This emotional episode follows chimpanzees rescued from testing labs as they adapt to new lives of freedom and care.

programme.title: Stung by the Dragon Flame Caterpillar!
programme.desc: The dragon flame caterpillar may be tiny, but its sting is fiery. Coyote gets up close—and pays the price.Even plants can pack a punch. Coyote explores a Costa Rican bush with stinging leaves that deliver an unexpectedly sharp pain.

programme.title: Mini Bulls Don't Have Mini Horns!
programme.desc: They're tiny, they're horned, and they're tougher than they look. Coyote introduces us to mini bulls—small cattle with big personalities. Mark gets another jolt—this time on purpose—from an electric eel. He demonstrates how the animal uses electricity to navigate and defend.

programme.title: Blippi
programme.desc: Blippi visits a magic store to learn some magic tricks and entertain his friends!

programme.title: Blippi
programme.desc: It's time for fun in the sun, getting down in the dirt, and cooling off at the end of the long day with frozen treats. YUM!

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring
programme.desc: As little ones become increasingly mobile, their fascination with all things that move intensifies too. On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring exposes babies to the different ways people travel -- on land, by sea, and in the air -- while it presents them with captivating real-world images, funny puppet shows, and beautiful classical music by Beethoven, Haydn, Mozart and Rossini.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: From the moment babies are born, they use their developing senses to discover the world. In terms of sight, bold patterns in contrasting colors or black and white are initially preferred. As for sounds, Mother s gentle voice, in any language, is music to baby's ears. Language Nursery: Voices From Many Lands acquaints your baby with the sounds of foreign language by presenting delightful, visually stimulating images accompanied by spoken passages, natural sounds and music.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby Einstein: Let's Learn about Classical Music
programme.desc: Discover instruments through narration and repetition. Real-life images and sounds will engage viewers as they dance to upbeat classical favorites from Beethoven, Mozart and more. Join your favorite Baby Einstein characters to discover what makes each instrument unique!

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: At 12 months, babies can identify specific sounds, and they re also starting to match the sounds they hear with the objects that make them. Meet the Orchestra -- First Instruments presents your little "maestro" with a playful introduction to the sights and sounds of a real orchestra -- from the clashing of cymbals to the purring of the cello and everything in between!

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Get moving with Baby Einstein! Real, live-action footage and classical melodies engage young viewers as they discover simple words related to movement. Enjoy encouraging melodies and live-action footage of babies and animals as you learn and move alongside your favorite Baby Einstein friends. Dance along as you watch real, live action footage of animals, children, and toys.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Early on in babies' development, they begin to recognize primary colors such as blue, red, and yellow. And all the while, they're also processing an infinite variety of new sights and sounds. Think of the joy you'll experience as you introduce your little one to beautiful music and imagery with Baby Beethoven Symphony of Fun.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Discover the exciting world of shapes and colors! Learn about the primary colors red, yellow, and blue with real-life imagery of animals and objects. Explore colors like purple, orange, and green and see artwork by Van Gogh! Discover the circles, squares, and triangles that surround us in everyday life, and introduce baby to the world of visual arts as children and artists create their own work!

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Babies love animals, and now you can bring animals from the seven continents home to your baby. Meet dozens of exotic animals including Asian pandas, Australian kangaroos and Arctic snow monkeys...all filmed in their beautiful, natural habitats. Rhythms and songs inspired by faraway lands have been orchestrated especially for little ears and encourage your baby to sing, dance and play to the world beats. You'll laugh together at the funny puppet shows featuring moms and their baby animals.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Baby Lullaby takes your baby on a soothing journey with classical lullabies by Bach, Brahms and Mozart. Enjoy relaxing ocean waves, sweet puppet shows and tranquil images. From fun time to rest time, Baby Lullaby is the perfect way to spend special time with your baby.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: World Music introduces you and your little one to beautiful sounds from every continent, bringing a fresh, new experience of music and culture. Sing, dance and play to exotic rhythms that open the door to an appreciation of different cultures from around the world.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: At 12 months, babies can identify specific sounds, and they re also starting to match the sounds they hear with the objects that make them. Meet the Orchestra -- First Instruments presents your little "maestro" with a playful introduction to the sights and sounds of a real orchestra -- from the clashing of cymbals to the purring of the cello and everything in between!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Honey Pie Pony hurts her leg and must stay in bed instead of heading off for a grand adventure. A disappointed Honey proves to be a very impatient patient, that is until Strawberry Shortcake and her friends work together telling stories and...

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: The Baby Berrykins are enthralled by a fairytale that Blueberry reads to them for Story Time. Charmed by their reaction, Strawberry suggests that the girls do a "berrytale play" for the kids, sort of a "Story Time On Stage." Plum volunteers to direct it. She envisions a small, simple production-until she hears about a gigantic production of "Snowberry and the Seven Berrykins" that's a smash hit in Berry Big City.

programme.title: Sky's the Limit
programme.desc: When a storm causes a giant boulder to cut off Berry Bitty City's water supply, Strawberry and the girls face the possibility of having to move away from their homes. With the help of Mr. Longface Caterpillar, the girls set out on an adventure to save their town.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: While sipping cider in Strawberry's café, the girls muse about how they cannot wait for winter to arrive. They list the various traditions they love about the season but the one they all agree on is the Glimmerberry Ball. Strawberry mentions that she doesn't think the café can hold the party this year because of how the town has grown. The girls decide to split up and search for a new location.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry and the girls excitedly plan to put on a "Story Time On Stage" musical play for the Berrykin kids: "Berryella and Prince Berry Charming," directed and choreographed by Plum, with musical direction by Cherry. Strawberry will play Berryella, with Lemon and Blueberry as her comically jealous stepsisters, and costume designer Raspberry as her Fairy Fashionmother.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry's cat Custard is shown to live a pampered, adored existence in Strawberry's house. But Custard's dream set-up is shattered by the sudden arrival of a rambunctious stray puppy, Pupcake, who Strawberry takes in during a storm.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Conniving Peppermint Fizz talks the other kids into a competition to see whose pet is best. Peppermint cheats so that her pet, Cola Chameleon, wins best pet. But she is surprised when the other kids don't care who won.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Honey Pie Pony is feeling left out of all the kids' play. She's the only one with four legs. There's no one like her to talk to and share her feelings. So Strawberry and the others take Honey Pie to Ice Cream Island - a land of horses.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Honey Pie Pony is excited that the ponies of Ice Cream Island have invited her to take part in their annual Festival of the Horses.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: It's another windy winter day, and the postal bees are staying indoors-the wind is too strong for their berry bitty wings. "We don't know when the next mail will get through!" It's bad news for Blueberry, who has been anxiously awaiting the next installment of a Patty Persimmon Detective story. If only she could get to the post office in Berry Bitty Dale where her package is waiting!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: The Baby Berrykins are enthralled by a fairytale that Blueberry reads to them for Story Time. Charmed by their reaction, Strawberry suggests that the girls do a "berrytale play" for the kids, sort of a "Story Time On Stage." Plum volunteers to direct it. She envisions a small, simple production-until she hears about a gigantic production of "Snowberry and the Seven Berrykins" that's a smash hit in Berry Big City.

programme.title: Blippi's Fun and Colorful Exploration
programme.desc: Get ready for a vibrant and entertaining escapade with Blippi as he dives into a world of colors at Billy Beez, creates a whimsical pizza in the kitchen, transforms into a playful pirate on a treasure hunt, and explores the fascinating world of dinosaurs at the Santa Barbara Museum of Natural History. Each adventure is packed with laughter, creativity, and educational moments, making learning a joyful experience for kids.

programme.title: The Berry Big Harvest
programme.desc: Finding she has some free time one morning, Strawberry visits Berrykin Bloom who is filled with excitement over his new crop of Berry Bitty fruits and vegetables his garden has never grown so much produce! He can't wait to share his new crop (the perfect bitty size) with Berry Bitty City, so Strawberry volunteers to help him transport the produce to Orange's General Store. Soon Orange's store is overflowing with Berry Bitty Fruits & Veggies. It's the berry first time Orange has not been able to find a “place for everything” in her store.

programme.title: Room at the Top
programme.desc: Strawberry is really touched when her girlfriends and the Berrykins volunteer to help her with the construction of her new marketplace, so when the girls suggest they take charge of designing Strawberry's dream-bedroom to go on the second story, of course Strawberry agrees. While construction is going on, the girls each show Strawberry their individual ideas for the bedroom. Grateful for the support and not wanting to dampen anyone's enthusiasm, Strawberry tells each of them yes (actually, more like not-no).

programme.title: Don't Forget the Leaves!
programme.desc: Sid and his friends investigate and discover that leaves are food for animals, they provide shade, and they help make nutrients so flowers and trees can grow!

programme.title: The Bug Club
programme.desc: Sid and his friends use their magnifying glasses and discover ants, rolie polies, and caterpillars around the playground.

programme.title: Sky's the Limit
programme.desc: When a storm causes a giant boulder to cut off Berry Bitty City's water supply, Strawberry and the girls face the possibility of having to move away from their homes. With the help of Mr. Longface Caterpillar, the girls set out on an adventure to save their town.

programme.title: Blippi's Great Green Adventure
programme.desc: In this special episode, Blippi celebrates Earth Day with his Great Green Adventure. Caring for the environment is one of our prime responsibilities and Blippi teaches children how by taking them on a puzzle-solving adventure.

programme.title: Starlight, Star Bright
programme.desc: With time running out before the Grand Opening celebration for the new Marketplace, Strawberry's friends get the idea to invite her favorite singer, superstar CHERRY JAM, to be the guest singer. The girls are blown away when their berryjuice cell phone gets a text message that Cherry accepts their invitation! Strawberry is touched by their surprise, but reminds them that now the town and Marketplace really have to shine! The girls and Berrykins rush to turn the Marketplace Café into a suitable performance area for a big star like Cherry Jam.

programme.title: Practice Makes Perfect
programme.desc: Cherry Jam is so enjoying staying in Berry Bitty City, that she calls her managers in Big Bitty City and informs them that she is going to stay for a much needed vacation! Strawberry and the girls are thrilled that she will be with them for a little longer, and they do all they can to make their guest comfortable. But Cherry doesn't want to be treated like a guest she wants to fit in and contribute to the community. Plum suggests that she teach music lessons Plum has always wanted to learn the drums, as it would enhance her ability to give dance lessons with live rhythmic accompaniment.

programme.title: Link into the VRAINS
programme.desc: n Den City, duels are held in the highly advanced VR world Link VRAINS, which is always under attack by the Knights of Hanoi. However, a hero named Playmaker has arrived to face the Knights, capturing a program they want called an Ignis.

programme.title: Seize the Wind!
programme.desc: Playmaker continues his duel with the Knight of Hanoi using the new Speed Duel rules. Playmaker is backed into a corner by the Knight's Cracking Dragon, but a data storm soon envelopes the field and Playmaker activates his Skill.

programme.title: Blippi's Summer Games
programme.desc: Blippi is back with Blippi's Sports Summer Games Movie For Kids!

programme.title: Talking Tom and Friends
programme.desc: Armed with tech gear and an unfailing sense of optimism, Talking Tom and Friends are setting out to make their dreams come true!

programme.title: Talking Tom and Friends
programme.desc: Friendship goes bad when Talking Tom's happy customer service representative turns into a villain.

programme.title: Atomic Betty
programme.desc: Atomic Betty - Evil Juniors / As the Worm Turns: Betty is called off to a mission where she must teach young Galactic Guardians in training the power of responsibility but it's all a front for Maximus' latest scheme to eliminate Betty./ Maximus is using a powerful telescope-like device to destroy asteroids and even some planets and Betty must stop Maximus before he succeeds in destroying entire planets and eventually the Galactic Guardian HQ.

programme.title: Atomic Betty
programme.desc: Atomic Betty - Extreme Makeover: An evil villainess named Bombshelle is committing fashion-related crimes across the galaxy while, on Earth, Betty's band shoots their first music video./ Zulia unwittingly helps Max Sr escape from prison! Meanwhile, Purrsy is celebrating a birthday.

programme.title: Rainbow Rangers
programme.desc: Rainbow Rangers - Teacup Tigers / Glass Warfare: When Preston tries to shrink his own "teacup" tigers to take home to Patty, the Rangers have to stop him and restore the tigers to their proper size! / The Rangers save an entire desert and its inhabitants from Priscilla, who's trying to turn it all into her new glass palace.

programme.title: Rainbow Rangers
programme.desc: Rainbow Rangers - Who Said Bat: The Rangers need to help bats from colliding with wind turbines. / Pollution from a gold mine threatens the otters in the ocean.

programme.title: Angry Birds
programme.desc: Angry Birds - Stalker, Photochucked & More: Fun adventure episodes from Angry Birds, Piggy Tales and Stella! In this episode watch "Stalker", "Photochucked" and more!

programme.title: Angry Birds
programme.desc: Angry Birds - Slingshot 101 & More: Fun adventure episodes from Angry Birds, Piggy Tales and Stella! In this episode watch "Slingshot 101", "Thunder Chuck" and more!

programme.title: Super Mario Bros 3
programme.desc: Super Mario Bros 3 - Up, up and a Koopa: Kooky uses his new "Raiser Upper" machine to levitate everything in the Mushroom Kingdom to the sky. / Another set of adventures starring the now-classic kids' heroes, The Super Marios Bros. This time Koopa has escaped from a Banishment Zone to battle the Super Mario Bros. And he has a powerful new weapon, the Koopa Kids - Koopa's bratty, yet lovable.

programme.title: Super Mario Bros 3
programme.desc: Super Mario Bros 3 - Mush Rumors / The Ugly Mermaid: When a real world family drives into the Mushroom Kingdom, the Mushroom People and the Koopas mistake them for aliens. / While wearing a Frog Suit, Mario almost drowns, but he is saved by Holly Mackerel, a mermaid princess. Holly falls in love with Mario and tries to marry him.

programme.title: Shaq's Garage
programme.desc: Shaq's Garage - Roadsterball / Blond is Back!: Klaus' longtime nemesis, Niccolo Tessio, challenges the Paq to a game of Roadsterball. Team leader Klaus only chooses the fastest and strongest of the Paq to help, but it doesn't go the way he planned. Klaus must figure out a way to utilize all of the strengths of the Paq members or they'll loose the game. / Shaq has a big surprise for Fleming. He's invited the actor who played James Blond, Ian Connery, to come visit. Ian always wished they could have gone on a real mission together and Fleming feels the same way.

programme.title: Shaq's Garage
programme.desc: Shaq's Garage - Close Encounters of the Bad Kind / Gold Fever!: Dawkins and Himari are driving back to the garage at night and he is pointing out different stars to her and she is bored. Dawkins sees a strange star that he's never seen before. Then, the star moves across the sky and crashes in the distance. An alien captures them, intending to take them back to his planet as spare parts and they must find a way to escape before the ship takes off. / Boone tells The Paq about the story of The Lost Scotchman's Mine, a legendary treasure buried deep in the hills or Orlanta. Is it a myth or real? Fleming, Gronk, and Himari all decide to find out.

programme.title: Superhero Kindergarten
programme.desc: Superhero Kindergarten - Time Trippers: The Super K's accidental trip through time teaches everyone that history is best left in the past.

programme.title: Holiday Gift Swap Disaster
programme.desc: While the rest of the group gets creative and makes new, personalized gifts from scratch. In the end, it's a reminder that it's not about the gifts—it's about the thought and effort you put into showing you care.

programme.title: Day Of The Great Devourer
programme.desc: Disaster threatens as the ninja must fight to defeat the all consuming Great Devourer in a desperate attempt to save Ninjago.

programme.title: LEGO Marvel Super Heroes: Avengers Reassembled
programme.desc: The Avengers must now fight one of their own, and they're not sure what to do! Cap and Thor lead the charge, but can't fend off Iron Man and Ultron's Minions. Meanwhile, Black Widow, Hawkeye and Hulk track down a signal that seems to be controlling Iron Man from a castle in the “Little Sokovia” section of New York. There they fight HYDRA and BARON VON STRUCKER and learn that YELLOWJACKET is the “bug” that infected Iron Man's suit for Ultron. To help break Iron Man free from Ultron's control, the Avengers call in their own bug...ANT MAN! Finally, the Avengers confront Ultron and his Ultron Minions as they attack Avengers' Tower. Ultron's ultimate plan: use all the data/stuff they've stolen to free the newly rebuilt IRON LEGION, destroy the Avengers, and control the world. Tony dons the hastily repaired Hulk Buster Armor and it's on! It's a huge battle as the Avengers try to save their beloved HQ and defeat Ultron. Will they succeed? And, more importantly, can their party be saved after Ultron tries to take down their Tower?The Avengers prepare for a party with a surprise guest when Ultron crashes the preparations by using mind control on Iron Man. While trying to rescue their leader, the rest of the team discovers that Baron Stucker, HYDRA, and Yellowjacket are in on Ultron's plans. While preparing for a party at Avengers' Tower, THE AVENGERS (IRON MAN, CAPTAIN AMERICA, BLACK WIDOW, HAWKEYE, THOR, HULK and VISION) notice Iron Man acting strangely. When Iron Man volunteers to go out and get more ice, they find it odd: when has Tony every volunteered to get anything? Suddenly, ULTRON appears on a screen: he's taken control of Iron Man! To make matters worse, Ultron takes over all the versions of Iron Man's armor and they fly off!

programme.title: The Book Of Obsession
programme.desc: The Knights are tasked with protecting the King's valuable Ned Knights comic book during the Bookingheim Knight-a-Con. But with all the costumed kids there, will they recognize the real Jestro and Book of Monsters coming to get them? King Halbert instructs the Knights that they must take his valuable Ned Knights comic book to the “Knight-a-Kon” in Bookingham. It will be the first be first prize at the Knight-a-Kon. But the Book of Monsters wants the book, too. Having devoured Ned Knightly comic books in it's down time it has become fanatically obsessed with the storyline. General Magmar suggests they simply attack the place and take what they want, but Jestro tells them they can't: everyone will be in costume and they'll be thousands of comics there. This calls for a clever “infiltration” mission (which will also allow Jestro to see the Kon, which he's always wanted to do.) So Jestro goes into the Kon with the BOM and some monsters. And it's easy because everyone is in costume Jestro, the BOM and their monsters fit right in at the convention and Clay and crew have their work cut out for them trying to separate fiend from fan. Macy keeps going on-and-on about the story in the King's comic book as the Knights keep a watchful eye on it. Robin even shows up in the “Minitrex” – a small version of the Fortrex he built just for the Knight-a-Kon. He's convinced he can win the costume competition that is the big closing event for the Kon. At the end of the Kon there's a big awards ceremony for “Best Costume.” The prize: “Ned Knightly, Knight of Knighton, Issue #1.” The BOM must get it and scheme to get closer to it during the costume contest. He's just…about…to get the book when…the impatient Magmar attacks! Everyone thinks it's part of the big show! “Ned Knightly, Knight of Knighton, Issue #1” is tossed from person-to-person-to-person in the melee. The Knights must do something, but they're too far away from the Fortrex to download any NEXO-Power. Robin pulls up in his Minitrex they can use it to get NEXO-Power! Robin wins the best costume for his Minitrex and wins the Ned Knightly Comic which he then gives to Macy…she deserves to read it.

programme.title: LEGO Marvel Super Heroes: Avengers Reassembled
programme.desc: The Avengers must now fight one of their own, and they're not sure what to do! Cap and Thor lead the charge, but can't fend off Iron Man and Ultron's Minions. Meanwhile, Black Widow, Hawkeye and Hulk track down a signal that seems to be controlling Iron Man from a castle in the “Little Sokovia” section of New York. There they fight HYDRA and BARON VON STRUCKER and learn that YELLOWJACKET is the “bug” that infected Iron Man's suit for Ultron. To help break Iron Man free from Ultron's control, the Avengers call in their own bug...ANT MAN! Finally, the Avengers confront Ultron and his Ultron Minions as they attack Avengers' Tower. Ultron's ultimate plan: use all the data/stuff they've stolen to free the newly rebuilt IRON LEGION, destroy the Avengers, and control the world. Tony dons the hastily repaired Hulk Buster Armor and it's on! It's a huge battle as the Avengers try to save their beloved HQ and defeat Ultron. Will they succeed? And, more importantly, can their party be saved after Ultron tries to take down their Tower?The Avengers prepare for a party with a surprise guest when Ultron crashes the preparations by using mind control on Iron Man. While trying to rescue their leader, the rest of the team discovers that Baron Stucker, HYDRA, and Yellowjacket are in on Ultron's plans. programme.title: EXO 'I'm Home' MV
programme.desc: “I'm Home” is a pop ballad that blends delicate piano melodies with lush strings, expressing the happiness of being reunited with loved ones and the wish to stay in this moment for a long time.EXO has hinted at a new winter song that follows the emotional resonance of the chart resurgence of “First Snow.”

programme.title: YOONA 'Wish to Wish' MV
programme.desc: 'Wish to Wish' is a medium-tempo pop song that reinterprets the mood of 1980s pop. The lyrics written by YOONA herself convey the heartfelt message of “let's continue to shine together for a long time,” giving the song an even more special meaning.The harmonious blend of YOONA's soft, calm vocal tone and its lively orchestral arrangement leaves a strong impression.

programme.title: RIIZE 'Fame' MV
programme.desc: The bold, forward-driving rhythmic structure combined with the raw texture of electric guitar creates a dynamic surge of energy. The lyrics reflect RIIZE's artistic vision, delivering the message that what they truly seek is not fame, but the sharing of emotions and love.RIIZE's second single “Fame” is the group's first venture into a rage-style hip-hop track, expressing their artistic ideals as “emotional pop artists.”

programme.title: TAEYEON 'Panorama' MV
programme.desc: TAEYEON celebrates the 10th anniversary of her solo debut with the compilation album Panorama, featuring the Pop Rock title track “인사 (Panorama).” The song pairs vibrant piano, synths, and a strong drum line with soaring guitar riffs and TAEYEON's delicate yet powerful vocals. The music video presents a cinematic story of TAEYEON, who has lost her memory, and a girl who helps restore it — a metaphor for the bond between the artist and her fans, SONEs. Its emotional, road-movie-style visuals reveal SONEs as the hidden protagonist. The 24-track album offers a retrospective of her past decade, unfolding like a panorama of her solo journey.

programme.title: EXO 'REVERXE' Trailer
programme.desc: At the end of the trailer, the title of EXO's 8th full-length album, “REVERXE,” was finally revealed. Based on EXO's universe storyline — “the day we truly become one, a new world will open” — the album name reinterprets the word “Reverse” in EXO's own style, carrying the meaning “a new world opens once again,” further heightening anticipation.EXO has returned with a new trailer.

programme.title: DOYOUNG 'Promise' MV
programme.desc: The title track “Promise” is an impressive ballad that begins with a minimal piano melody and gradually builds emotional depth as strings are added in the latter half. The lyrics, written by DOYOUNG, capture the feelings he couldn't easily express as his love grew deeper. Even if the confession comes late, it delivers a sincerity and emotional resonance stronger than any other words.

programme.title: Charles Lloyd, Keith Jarrett, Cecil McBee & Jack DeJohnette
programme.sub-title: Charles Lloyd Quartet live in Brussels
programme.desc: Since 1960, the legendary saxophonist Charles Lloyd has lent his adventurous, lyrical sound to collaborations with likes of Cannonball Adderley, Keith Jarrett and The Beach Boys. His early and highly successful experiments blending jazz and rock music charted a path that led to the electric fusion of Miles Davis and The Headhunters, while his exploration of spirituality and transcendental meditation added a new depth to his sound. Lloyd moves from hard swing to subtle ballads, blending gorgeous textures and Eastern influences. This 1966 recording features Lloyd's quartet, accompanied by Keith Jarrett (piano), Cecil McBee (double bass) and Jack DeJohnette (drums), playing some of Lloyd's own compositions.

programme.title: Jaco Pastorius
programme.desc: The audience at the Montreal Jazz Festival in Canada was in for a treat on July 3, 1982, as a true musical innovator hit the stage: Jaco Pastorius, who transformed the electric bass into a formidable solo instrument. Combining intricate harmonics, fluid melodies, and unparalleled technical skill, Pastorius left a lasting impact on the music world. In this concert, he is accompanied by Peter Erskine (drums), Don Alias (percussion), Othello Molineaux (steel drums), Bob Mintzer (tenor sax and bass clarinet), and Randy Brecker (trumpet and electronics). They perform “Chicken”, “Donna Lee”, “Mr. Phone Bone”, and “Fannie Mae”. Don't miss this extraordinary opportunity to experience Jaco Pastorius's electrifying performance at the Montreal Jazz Festival 1982!

programme.title: Cannonball Adderley
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: McCoy Tyner, Avery Sharpe & Louis Hayes
programme.sub-title: McCoy Tyner Trio live at Estival Lugano
programme.desc: Since 1977, Estival is a summer jazz festival in Switzerland, Lugano. Estival offers a thrilling and particularly surprising line-up that explores the rich world of contemporary music whilst promoting the understanding of different cultures, tolerance, and co-existence. McCoy Tyner's first main exposure came with Benny Golson, being the first pianist in Golson's and Art Farmer's Jazztet. He's also known for his work with the John Coltrane Quartet. Tyner's style is easily comparable to Coltrane's maximalist style of saxophone. Though a member of Coltrane's group, he was never overshadowed by the saxophonist, but complemented and even inspired Coltrane's open-minded approach. McCoy Tyner is considered one of the most influential jazz pianists of the 20th century, an honour he earned both with Coltrane and in his years of performing following Coltrane's death.

programme.title: Chucho Valdis
programme.desc: Legendary Cuban pianist Chucho Valdés brought his band ‘Jazz Batá 2' to the Malta Jazz Festival on July 19, 2019. The air was thick with anticipation as Valdés took to the stage, revisiting the small-group concept of his mythical 1972 Cuban album ‘Jazz Batá'. The batá repertoire – the deep classical music of West Africa – permeates Valdés' piano solos throughout the concert. Valdés's band consists of Dreiser Durruthy (Batas and vocals), Yaroldy Abreu (percussions and vocals), Abraham Mansfarroll (percussion), Ramón Vazquez Martirena-Bajo (bass guitar). They delivered a performance that reverberated through the historic island.

programme.title: Jack DeJohnette, Howard Johnson, Greg Osby & John Purcell
programme.sub-title: Jack DeJohnette's Special Edition at Estival 1985
programme.desc: Since 1977, Estival is a summer jazz festival in Switzerland, Lugano. Estival offers a thrilling and particularly surprising line-up that explores the rich world of contemporary music whilst promoting the understanding of different cultures, tolerance, and co-existence. Jack DeJohnette, an important figure in jazz fusion, was inducted into the Modern Drummer Hall of Fame in 2007. His style combines elements of jazz, free jazz, world music and R & B, and his versatility makes him a regarded and in-demand drummer. Discover his unique style as part of this breathtaking performance delivered at Estival.

programme.title: Marion Williams
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Paul Desmond, Dave Brubeck & Joe Morello
programme.sub-title: Dave Brubeck and Paul Desmond live in Belgium
programme.desc: Dave Brubeck: Live in '64 boasts a beautifully filmed concert from one of the most beloved quartets in jazz history. Captured at the pinnacle of their power and popularity, Paul Desmond (alto sax), Joe Morello (drums), Eugene Wright (bass) and Dave Brubeck (piano) explore the trails they blazed into the realm of odd time signatures with ‘Forty Days' and two versions of their groundbreaking hit ‘Take Five', as well as forays into world music with a unique exploration of ‘Koto Song'. Their intimate onstage chemistry and impeccable musicianship made the DBQ an award-winning jazz supergroup.

programme.title: Buck Clayton, Henry Chaix, Théo Siegrist & Pierre Bouru
programme.desc: In the second half of June 1965, during a prolonged European concert tour, American trumpeter Buck Clayton (1911–1991) played a residency at the Chikito, a club in Bern, Switzerland. With a local rhythm section of French pianist Henry Chaix, Swiss double bassist Théo Siegrist, and Swiss drummer Pierre Bouru, this stalwart of the Count Basie Orchestra gave a sparkling performance that was documented for posterity by Radio Télévision Suisse in the program ‘Variétés'. Opening with the George Gershwin favorite ‘'S Wonderful', the program continues with two swing-era classics: ‘I Can't Get Started' and ‘Honeysuckle Rose'. Revel at how easily Clayton finishes ‘Honeysuckle Rose' on a high concert F! A spirited romp through Juan Tizol's composition ‘Perdido' closes this recording. The success of this booking ensured Clayton received repeated warm receptions in Bern in the following decades.

programme.title: Miles Davis
programme.desc: American trumpeter Miles Davis (1926-1991) was a pioneering jazz musician from the late 1940s to the late 1980s. His influence on 20th century music is hard to overstate, as he trailblazed bebop, cool jazz, hardbop, modal jazz, and electronic music. On October 11, 1964, Miles Davis performed at Teatro Dell'Arte in Milan. With tenor saxophonist Wayne Shorter, pianist Herbie Hancock, bassist Ron Carter and drummer Tony Williams, Davis performed Autumn Leaves, My Funny Valentine, All Blues, All of You, and Joshua.

programme.title: Wes Montgomery
programme.sub-title: Herbie Man & Sonny Sharrock - Memphis underground
programme.desc: This selection of unforgettable performances by some of the most iconic and influential jazz guitarists in history showcases these legends' unique essence. Revel in Wes Montgomery's virtuosity as he performs the captivating “Nica's Dream” in Holland in 1965, and delight in the expressive beauty of his rendition of “Here's That Rainy Day”, recorded in London in that same year. Experience the timeless appeal of Joe Pass's “You Stepped Out of a Dream” and “You Turned The Tables on Me”, recorded live in Hanover in 1975. The interplay between Jim Hall and Atilla Zoller borders on the impossible as they perform “Extensions” in Hanover in 1973. Enjoy Jim Hall's lyrical solo performance of “I'm Getting Sentimental Over You” from 1964. Witness Sonny Sharrock's avant-garde fusion of jazz and rock in an exhilarating performance of “Peanut", recorded live in France in 1970. Sharrock is joined by flutist Herbie Mann in a soulful performance of “Memphis Underground” from 1969. Immerse yourself in the magic of jazz guitar!

programme.title: Anita Oday, Göran Engdahl, Roman Dylag & John Poole
programme.desc: Anita O'Day boasts two wonderful concerts from 1963 and 1970 that present the “jazz singer supreme” in impeccable form. O'Day's horn-based approach to singing is in full effect throughout both shows including stand-out renditions in each show of audience favorites “Tea For Two” and “Sweet Georgia Brown” (both reprised from her triumphant appearance at the 1958 Newport Jazz Festival.) This show is a fitting testimony to one of jazz music's true originals and shows unequivocally why she is ranked in the top tier of vocalists along with Ella Fitzgerald, Sarah Vaughan, and Billie Holiday.

programme.title: Jos van Immerseel, Anima Eterna Brugge, Claron McFadden & Bart Van Caenegem
programme.desc: Belgian conductor Jos van Immerseel leads Anima Eterna Brugge in a concert program dedicated to American composer George Gershwin. The program opens with Gershwin's symphonic suite Catfish Row (arr. Steven D. Bowen) which is based upon music from his famous opera Porgy and Bess (1935). This is followed by the well-known tone poem An American in Paris (1928). After, soprano Claron McFadden joins the orchestra, presenting a selection of Gershwin's classic jazz songs, including ‘The man I love' (1924-27), ‘I got rhythm' (1930), and ‘By Strauss' (1936). She also performs ‘My man's gone now' and ‘Summertime' from Porgy and Bess. The concert ends with Gershwin's celebrated Rhapsody in Blue, with Bart Van Caenegem as featured pianist. This performance was recorded at Concertgebouw Brugge, Belgium, on March 3, 2017.

programme.title: Vasilii Andreev
programme.desc: The award-winning Imperial Ice Stars are 26 Olympic, World, European and National Championship skaters, who jointly hold over 250 competition medals, performing theatrical ice dancing on a frozen theatre stage. The Imperial Ice Stars have received five-star praise from critics and standing ovations from audiences at some of the world's most prestigious venues - London's Royal Albert Hall and Sadler's Wells, Singapore's Esplanade Theatre, Montreal's Place des Arts and Cape Town's Artscape. This stunning performance, filmed at the Melbourne Theatre Company in Australia, offers a new look on the secular tale of Sleeping beauty, with the original music of Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky (1840-1893).

programme.title: Ballet of the Mariinsky Theatre
programme.desc: The Mariinsky Ballet performs 'Anna Karenina', a sweeping two-act ballet choreographed by Alexei Ratmansky. The ballet is based on Lev Tolstoy's 1877 novel that tells the tragic story of the married socialite Anna Karenina and her affair with cavalry officer Count Vronsky. The ballet opens where the novel ends: tormented by despair and loneliness, Anna throws herself under a train, whereupon Count Vronsky discovers his lover's lifeless body. Ratmansky's choreography is set to Rodion Shchedrin's music, which is performed by the Mariinsky Orchestra under the baton of Valery Gergiev. Among the dancers are Ulyana Lopatkina, Victor Baranov, Andrei Yermakov, and Svetlana Ivanova. This performance was recorded at the Mariinsky Theatre in St. Petersburg, Russia, in March 2014.

programme.title: Roberto Abbado, Filarmonica Arturo Toscanini, Coro del Teatro Regio di Parma & Eleonora Buratto
programme.desc: Roberto Abbado conducts the Filarmonica Arturo Toscanini, the Coro del Teatro Regio di Parma, and four vocal soloists in an outstanding performance of Giuseppe Verdi's Messa da Requiem, recorded at the Parco Ducale di Parma, Italy as part of Festival Verdi 2020. The soloists are soprano Eleonora Buratto, mezzosoprano Anita Rachvelishvili, tenor Giorgio Berrugi, and bass Roberto Tagliavini. When his fellow composer Gioachino Rossini died in 1868, Verdi proposed to compose a ‘Messa per Rossini' in his honor, to be written by himself and several other Italian composers. Verdi wrote the concluding movement, ‘Libera me'. When the premiere was cancelled, the project lay dormant. When Italian poet Alessandro Manzoni passed away a few years later, Verdi revisited his plan to compose a requiem – this time in honor of the poet he so greatly admired. As an opera composer, Verdi knew better than anyone how to infuse the work with drama, as is evident in the revised version of his ‘Libera me'. The famous, powerful ‘Dies Irae' especially stands out, depicting the horrors of the Last Judgement.

programme.title: David Bowie
programme.desc: Exploring the life and career of musician and actor David Bowie, who returned to the top of the charts in 2013 with The Next Day, his first album in a decade. Music critics and DJs offer their thoughts on the London-born artist's colourful career and enduring success.

programme.title: Elton John
programme.desc: The stratospheric career of Elton John. The ‘Rocket Man' that made the piano player a rock star, this documentary follows a journey from budding songwriter, to his 70s peak as the one of the most prolific artists of his generation, his flamboyant costumes and classic songs and closing with being one of the very few musicians who can still top the charts 45 years into their career.

programme.title: Guns N' Roses
programme.sub-title: Live From The O2 Arena London
programme.desc: Watch this mega-production of Guns N' Roses performing live at the O2 Arena to its fans singing and vibrating to each song. Includes Paradise City, Sweet Child O' Mine, November Rain and many more.

programme.title: Coldplay
programme.desc: COLDPLAY Founder members, Chris Martin and Jonny Buckland started the band under the name Pectorals whilst studying together in London during the mid 1990s. Now one of the biggest bands in the world, and with a string of hit singles under their belt In 2014 Coldplay released their sixth album titled Ghost Stories. Journalist reveal the rise to fame of Coldplay.

programme.title: Ocean Vibes
programme.desc: Beneath the Sea a compilation of spectacular underwater images from around the world. Captured in exclusively in 8K digital, Beneath the Sea features an award-winning original score by Alan Williams.

programme.title: Ocean Vibes
programme.desc: Coral Kingdoms was filmed on the kaleidoscopic reefs of Indonesia and the Maldives Islands.

programme.title: Ocean Vibes
programme.desc: Forest in the Sea captures the unusual beauty of undersea kelp forests from Alaska to Southern California.

programme.title: Ocean Vibes
programme.desc: Bejeweled Fishes captures the spectacular beauty of fishes inhabiting the coral reefs of the Tropical Pacific.

programme.title: Flying Over Spanish Islands
programme.desc: In their first collaboration, cinematographer Stefan Zimmerman and Nature Relaxation™ producer David Huting present "Above the Spanish Islands." This 50-minute 4K aerial film captures Mallorca and the Canary Islands, set to ambient music by Diamans, Electus, and Michael St. Laurent.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland: Part I" provides a soothing aerial journey through eastern Iceland's fjords and valleys, starting at sunrise in Brei_dalsvik. Enjoy views of mists, a waterfall, and the Brei_dals' river, set to nature sounds and music by The Cynic Project, showcasing East Iceland's beauty.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland 2
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland II: Summer in the Golden Circle" presents a serene aerial view of Iceland's south coast, starting at Skogafoss and moving to Vik, with black sand beaches, cliffs, and Vatnajökull glacier. Filmed in 2020, this 1-hour film features local wildlife and immersive music.

programme.title: Flying Over Kauai
programme.desc: "Flying Over Kauai" provides a soothing aerial journey over Hawaii's Garden Island, featuring the lush NaPali Coast, waterfalls, canyons, and beaches. Paired with new music by The Cynic Project and nature sounds, this 4K film offers a visually and sonically immersive experience.

programme.title: Flying Over Oahu
programme.desc: "Flying Over Oahu" offers a 97-minute aerial journey over Oahu's iconic coastlines with nature sounds and music by Alex Smith (The Cynic Project). From Waikiki Beach to Turtle Bay, enjoy breathtaking 4K landscapes, ending with a stunning Waikiki sunset.

programme.title: Flying Over Spanish Islands
